zh.lifeSummary: 當Heinrich Bach II.於大約1280年出生在德國貝也奧斯包格奧斯包格時，他的父親Heinrich I. Bach36歲，母親Adelheid von Hohenlohe Brauneck Neuhaus39歲。他於大約1305年與Gertrud結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他在德國貝也奧斯包格奧斯包格被列為bürgermeister 。他去世於1334年10月之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在德國貝也奧斯包格奧斯包格。

nameMeanings.name: Bach
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Johann, Alfons, Bernhard, Erna, Ernst.

German: topographic name for someone who lived by a stream, Middle High German bach ‘stream’. This surname is also found in some other European countries, most notably in Denmark and France (Alsace and Lorraine), but also in e.g. the Netherlands, Czechia (see also 5 below), and Croatia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Bach ‘stream, creek’.

variants.name: Bage
meanings: English (Durham and Yorkshire): possibly from Middle English bagge, Anglo-Norman French bage ‘badge, cognizance, emblem’, perhaps denoting someone who wore one as a sign of affiliation to a family or guild.
English: habitational name from The Bage in Dorstone, The Bage Farm in Madley, or Bage in Kington, all in Herefordshire. These are all names derived from Old English bæce ‘stream’; compare Bach 3.
French: variant of Bague, from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago, a short form of compound names based on the element bag ‘to fight’.

variants.name: Batch
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Bach   3.

variants.name: Baughan
meanings: Welsh: from the adjective bychan ‘little’, a diminutive of bach (see Bach 4), originally in general used to distinguish father and son bearing the same name in genealogies. This word usually appears in Anglicized surnames in the lenited form Vaughan .
History: A Welsh family of this name was established from an early date in Great Rollright, Oxfordshire, England. The name was probably brought to North America from Oxfordshire rather than Wales.

variants.name: Bartholomew
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bertilmew, Bertelmy, a borrowing of the Old French form of the Biblical personal name Bartholomew (from Latin Bartholomaeus; Hebrew ‘son of Talmai’, said to mean ‘having many furrows’, i.e. rich in land). This was an extremely popular personal name in Christian Europe, with many vernacular derivatives. It derived its popularity from the apostle Saint Bartholomew (Matthew 10:3), who was, among other things, the patron saint of tanners, vintners, and butlers.
As an Irish name, it has been used as an Anglicized form of Mac Pharthaláin (see McFarlane ).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

variants.name: Baugh
meanings: Welsh: probably an Anglicized form of Bach , from Welsh bach ‘little’.
Americanized form of German   Bach  .

variants.name: Baechle
meanings: South German (Bächle): Swabian-Alemannic variant of Bach , from a diminutive of Middle High German bach ‘stream’. Compare Bachle and Beckley .

variants.name: Bash
meanings: English: variant of   Bach   3.

Jewish: acronymic surname from the first letters of Hebrew ben ‘son of’ and of a personal name such as Shelomo (see Solomon ), Shemuel (see Samuel ), Shimon (see Simon ) or Shimshon (see Samson ); see also Brill . In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the variant Basch .
Americanized form of German   Basch  .

variants.name: Bache
meanings: German and English: variant of   Bach   1, 3.
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Bartholomaeus (see Bach 6, Bartholomew ).
East German: occupational name for a butcher from Middle High German bache ‘ham, smoked slab of bacon’.

variants.name: Bachofer
meanings: German: topographic name for a farmer living by a stream (see   Bach   and   Hofer  ).

nameMeanings.name: Heinrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from heim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power’. See Henry .

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

en.lifeSummary: When Heinrich Bach II. was born about 1280, in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bavaria, Germany, his father, Heinrich I. Bach, was 36 and his mother, Adelheid von Hohenlohe Brauneck Neuhaus, was 39. He married Gertrud about 1305. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. His occupation is listed as bürgermeister  in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bavaria, Germany. He died before October 1334, in his hometown, and was buried in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bavaria, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heinrich Bach II. est né environ 1280, à Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Heinrich I. Bach, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Adelheid von Hohenlohe Brauneck Neuhaus, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Gertrud environ 1305. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est bürgermeister  à Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Allemagne. Il est décédé avant octobre 1334, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Heinrich Bach II. um 1280 in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Heinrich I. Bach 36 und seine Mutter Adelheid von Hohenlohe Brauneck Neuhaus 39. Er hat um 1305 Gertrud geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als bürgermeister  in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Deutschland aufgeführt. Er starb vor Oktober 1334 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich Bach II. è nato il circa 1280 nel Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Germania, il suo padre, Heinrich I. Bach, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Adelheid von Hohenlohe Brauneck Neuhaus, aveva 39 anni. Lui sposato Gertrud circa 1305. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come bürgermeister  nel Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Germania. Morì prima del ottobre 1334, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Heinrich Bach II.が約1280年にドイツバイエルンアウグスブルグアウグスブルグで生まれた時，父Heinrich I. Bachは36歳，母Adelheid von Hohenlohe Brauneck Neuhausは39歳であった。約1305年にGertrudと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。ドイツバイエルンアウグスブルグアウグスブルグでの職業はbürgermeister と記録されている。彼は1334年10月以前に故郷で死去し，ドイツバイエルンアウグスブルグアウグスブルグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Heinrich Bach II.(이)가 대략 1280년 독일 베이른 오그스버그 오그스버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Heinrich I. Bach(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelheid von Hohenlohe Brauneck Neuhaus(은)는 39세였다. 그는 대략 1305년 Gertrud(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 독일 베이른 오그스버그 오그스버그에서 bürgermeister 이었다. 그는 1334년 10월 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 독일 베이른 오그스버그 오그스버그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich Bach II. nasceu aproximadamente 1280, em Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemanha, seu pai, Heinrich I. Bach, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Adelheid von Hohenlohe Brauneck Neuhaus, tinha 39 anos. Ele casou-se com Gertrud aproximadamente 1305. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como bürgermeister  em Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemanha. Ele faleceu antes outubro de 1334, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heinrich Bach II. родился около 1280 г. в местности аугсбург, аугсбург, беиирн, Германия, его отцу по имени Heinrich I. Bach было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelheid von Hohenlohe Brauneck Neuhaus, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Gertrud около 1305 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери.  Он работал по профессии bürgermeister  в местности аугсбург, аугсбург, беиирн, Германия. Он умер перед октябрь 1334 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности аугсбург, аугсбург, беиирн, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Heinrich Bach II. nació aproximadamente 1280, en Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemania, su papá, Heinrich I. Bach, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Adelheid von Hohenlohe Brauneck Neuhaus, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Gertrud aproximadamente 1305. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era bürgermeister  en Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemania. Murió antes de octubre de 1334, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemania.
